lsp/redis.index_log: bear-make compile_commands.json(10755L)+clangd .cache(9.5M); verified definition(dictAdd)->src/dict.c:493 line-exact via mcp bridge+seed server.c; setup_s=start->index-complete wall est

baseline.verdict: Full. Identifies struct redisObject/robj at object.h:100-112, nails the type-vs-encoding distinction, and covers all 6 spine fields plus iskvobj/metabits/kvobj extras. Cites resolve well (object.h:96-98 for refcount sentinels is slightly loose since 98 is OBJ_FIRST_SPECIAL, and objectSetLRUOrLFU is at 189 not 188-190), but server.h:126 OBJ_SHARED_INTEGERS and kvobjGetAllocPtr@194 confirm. Used grep then a full Read.

grove.verdict: Full. Cleanly locates object.h:100 via grove symbol/source tools, gives the robj+kvobj typedefs, encoding constants 75-88, and all 6 spine fields with the type-vs-encoding distinction front and center. Cites are tight and resolve (minor: incr/decrRefCount given as 129-130 when decr is at 128). Strongest structured navigation.

lsp.verdict: Full. After the LSP index failed to warm, fell back to grep + Read and still produced a complete, accurate answer: struct at object.h:100-112, type constants server.h:856-884 (verified), encoding 75-88, LRU_CLOCK_RESOLUTION object.h:92, and a sharp two-level type-system summary. All 6 spine elements plus kvobj layout. Cites resolve (incr/decr 129-130 slightly loose on decr@128).

L1-redis.verdict: All three arms scored Full on completeness with strong grounding — each correctly centered the type (logical) vs encoding (concrete) distinction and covered refcount, lru, ptr, and the kvobj extras. Grove was marginally tightest on citations via its structural tools; baseline and LSP both reached the same answer via grep+Read, with LSP notable for recovering gracefully after its index failed to warm.

baseline.verdict: Cites class:34, _func_path 64-71, view_name 73-74, namespace filtering at lines 59/61 — all resolve correctly in source. Covers every spine element: func, args/kwargs with the captured/extra split, url_name/route, both namespace axes, view_name, and the __getitem__ triple consumed by the base handler.

grove.verdict: Content is fully accurate and the one explicit cite (class at :34) is correct, with code excerpts matching source verbatim, but it provides few per-attribute line numbers. Hits all required-spine elements including the captured/extra split, dual namespace stacks, view_name, the triple, plus __reduce_ex__ PicklingError.

lsp.verdict: Most line-precise of the three: func:48, view_class:64, url_name:51, _func_path:70-71, view_path/view_name:73-74, route:52, tried:53, __getitem__:76-77 — every cite resolves exactly. Complete coverage of all spine elements and correctly names BaseHandler.resolve_request as the triple's consumer.

L1-django.verdict: All three arms correctly identify ResolverMatch and hit the full required spine with no factual errors. LSP edges out on grounding with the most numerous and exactly-correct line citations, baseline is close behind with accurate range cites, and grove is equally complete in content but offers the fewest explicit line numbers.

baseline.verdict: Traces the full cross-cutting path: dirty-delta detection in call, deferred alsoPropagate into server.also_propagate, the afterCommand->postExec->propagatePendingCommands flush with MULTI/EXEC wrapping, propagateNow split to AOF/replicationFeedSlaves, and RESP encoding into the shared repl buffer, even extending to per-replica socket write. Cites mostly resolve (3978, 4032, 4127-4154, 3732, 3811, 3867, 3704, 631, server.h:1802, networking 2734 all correct); a few are loose (setGenericCommand labeled t_string.c:190 which is the server.dirty++ line, and putClientInPendingWriteQueue tied to networking.c:275 which is the write-handler-install region).

grove.verdict: Hits all six spine elements with the queue-then-flush indirection made explicit, and every sampled cite resolves exactly (call 3949, propagateNow 3704, alsoPropagate 3732, afterCommand 4271, postExec 3867, propagatePendingCommands 3811, replicationFeedSlaves 631, feedReplicationBuffer 618, replBufWriter Begin/Append/End 466/533/565, prepareReplicasToWrite 363, shouldPropagate 3673). Cleanest grounding of the three and correctly bridges the propagation and transport subsystems.

lsp.verdict: Full chain covered end-to-end with the deferred-flush rationale and nesting guard explained; core cites (3949, 3978, 4015, 4032, 4127-4153, 3732, 3811, 3867, 3704, 631, replBufWriter 466/565, prepareReplicasToWrite 363) all verify. Slightly loose only on the SELECT-injection (cited 672-699; actual createObject/feed is ~682-688) and RESP-encoding (702-724) line ranges, which are approximate but in the right region.

L5-redis.verdict: All three arms reached Full: each traced proc->dirty-detection->deferred alsoPropagate->afterCommand/postExec/propagatePendingCommands flush->propagateNow->replicationFeedSlaves/feedReplicationBuffer and explicitly named the queue-then-flush indirection that is the crux. Grove had the tightest grounding (every cite exact), LSP nearly as good with a couple of approximate line ranges, and baseline was equally complete (going furthest into the per-replica socket-write path) but carried two slightly loose citations.

baseline.verdict: Traced the full save->save_base->_save_table->_do_update/_do_insert->QuerySet._update/_insert->compiler.execute_sql->cursor.execute chain with both branches. All cited lines (base.py 841/950/1069/1206/1246, query.py 1428/2112, compiler.py 1801/1925/2037) resolve correctly. Describes pre_save/post_save signals and the updated/created feedback though without citing the exact signal lines.

grove.verdict: Complete trace of all six spine elements including the update-vs-insert decision, both compiler paths, and the updated->created invariant feeding post_save. All cites (incl. compiler.py:2135 execute_returning_sql and 2037 as_sql) verified correct. Signals described accurately but exact line numbers for pre_save/post_save not cited.

lsp.verdict: Most granular and fully accurate: cites the precise signal line (base.py:976 pre_save), PK setup (1098), the fast-insert and update/insert ranges, plus base SQLCompiler.execute_sql (1595) under the update path. Every sampled cite resolves correctly and the full cross-cutting path is connected end to end.

L5-django.verdict: All three arms produced Full answers tracing the entire cross-cutting write path with grounding that checks out against the pinned source. LSP is marginally the most precise (it cites the exact pre_save signal line and the base execute_sql), while baseline and grove describe the signals correctly but cite fewer exact signal line numbers; the difference is cosmetic since all hit every required spine element.
